identity.canonical_title: TORIC CODE LATTICE DIMENSION Canonical Statutory Formula: L_toric

theory.canonical_statement: [ID 918] TORIC CODE LATTICE DIMENSION Canonical Statutory Formula: $L_{toric} = \sqrt{153600/2} \times \sqrt{153600/2}$ Formal Technical Dissertation: Defines the dimensions of the square lattice required for topological error correction. The code distance and stabilizer generators are derived from this geometric constraint, mapped to periodic boundary conditions [15]. Source: [15]
theory.technical_interpretation: TORIC CODE LATTICE DIMENSION Canonical Statutory Formula: L_toric is an algebraic definition, written L_toric = √(153600/2) × √(153600/2). It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
